seoTitle: Upper Burringbar vs Upper Bylong — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Upper Burringbar and Upper Bylong. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Upper Burringbar vs Upper Bylong: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Upper Burringbar and Upper Bylong.
heading: Upper Burringbar vs Upper Bylong — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Upper Burringbar, New South Wales and Upper Bylong, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Upper Burringbar has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Upper Bylong (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Upper Burringbar or Upper Bylong safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Upper Burringbar has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Upper Bylong scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Upper Burringbar scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Upper Bylong scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Upper Burringbar or Upper Bylong?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Upper Burringbar has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
